claims.claimText: UCL Injury pathoanatomy centers on ucl, elbow-joint, radial-collateral-ligament and related stabilizers.

claims.claimText: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating ucl injury.

metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating ucl injury.

claims.claimText: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for ucl injury.

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for ucl injury.

claims.claimText: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for ucl injury — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for ucl injury — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

claims.claimText: UCL injuries in throwers present with medial elbow pain and valgus instability requiring graded return-to-throw planning.

metadata.claim_text: UCL injuries in throwers present with medial elbow pain and valgus instability requiring graded return-to-throw planning.

claims.claimText: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for ucl injury require attending-defined progression gates.

claims.claimText: Valgus stress testing and throwing history are central to UCL injury evaluation.

decisionPoints.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from ucl injury despite appropriate initial management
decisionPoints.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

decisionPoints.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after ucl injury treatment
decisionPoints.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones

decisionPoints.trigger: Throwing athlete with confirmed UCL tear and inability to return to sport after rehab
decisionPoints.action: Discuss UCL reconstruction (Tommy John) with graft selection and return-to-throw timeline
